title: Quick start guide to rpx
text: px_api() accepts short alias ("scb", "ssb", "statfi") full URL. Use px_api_catalogue() list known instances.
code: library(rpx)  scb <- px_api("scb", lang = "en") scb

title: Quick start guide to rpx
text: PX-Web organises data tables. table holds data cube one dimensions (called variables). Use get_tables() search: result tibble. can narrow client side table_search(), inspect tables table_describe(): table_describe() now shows subject path, time period range, data source alongside title — making much easier pick right table.
code: tables <- get_tables(scb, query = "population") tables tables |>   table_search("municipal") |>   table_describe(max_n = 3, format = "md")

title: Quick start guide to rpx
text: table ID, inspect variables (dimensions) : variable set available values (codes). Look specific variable’s values:
code: vars <- get_variables(scb, "TAB683") vars |> variable_describe() vars |> variable_values("Region")

title: Quick start guide to rpx
text: Now know variables table values available. Pass selections get_data(): ContentsCode tells API measure (population, deaths, etc.). "*" means “measures table”. Variables omit eliminated — API returns pre-computed aggregate (e.g., omitting Kon gives totals sexes). variables allow ; see vignette("introduction--rpx") mandatory vs eliminable. Selection helpers like px_top(), px_from(), px_range() let select values without knowing exact codes. Use want “latest N periods” “everything 2020 onward” rather typing specific year codes.
code: pop <- get_data(scb, "TAB638",   Region = c("0180", "1480"),   ContentsCode = "*",   Tid = px_top(5) ) pop

title: Quick start guide to rpx
text: can skip section prefer direct approach . prepare_query() inspects table fills sensible defaults — handy don’t want specify every variable: prints summary chosen . ’re happy, pass query get_data(): Set maximize_selection = TRUE automatically include many variables API’s cell limit allows:
code: q <- prepare_query(scb, "TAB638", Region = c("0180", "1480")) pop <- get_data(scb, query = q) q <- prepare_query(scb, "TAB638",   Region = c("0180"),   maximize_selection = TRUE )

title: Quick start guide to rpx
text: result standard tibble. Use favourite tidyverse tools: Notice _text suffix: get_data() returns raw code columns (Region = "0180") human-readable label columns (Region_text = "Stockholm"). Use _text columns display plotting; use raw codes filtering joining. useful helpers: data_minimize() — remove columns values identical data_legend() — generate caption string variable metadata px_cite() — create citation downloaded data
code: library(ggplot2)  pop |>   ggplot(aes(x = Tid, y = value, colour = Region_text)) +   # One line per region   geom_line(aes(group = Region_text)) +   # Separate panel for each measure (Population, Deaths, etc.)   facet_wrap(~ ContentsCode_text, scales = "free_y") +   # Rotate x-axis labels to avoid overlap   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1)) +   labs(     title = "Population over time",     caption = px_cite(pop)  # Auto-generated data citation   )

title: Quick start guide to rpx
text: Concepts & advanced features — vignette("introduction--rpx") covers data model, codelists, saved queries, query composition. Multiple countries — vignette("multi-api") shows compare data across national statistics agencies. ggplot2 reference — https://ggplot2-book.org/ visualisation.

title: Introduction to rpx
text: PX-Web statistical database platform used national statistics agencies across Nordic countries beyond. agency runs instance (Statistics Sweden scb.se, Statistics Norway ssb., etc.), share underlying API. rpx provides consistent, pipe-friendly R interface APIs. follows design principles rKolada: tibbles everywhere, search--fetch, progressive disclosure.

title: Introduction to rpx
text: Tibbles everywhere. Every function returns tibble (vector extracted one). Pipe-friendly. First argument always tibble API object; output always pipeable. Search--fetch. Users discover metadata downloading data. NULL failure. API errors return NULL warning, never stop(). Progressive disclosure. Simple things simple; complex things possible.

title: Introduction to rpx
text: PX-Web tables multi-dimensional data cubes. Unlike Kolada — dimensions always KPI, municipality, period — PX-Web table defines set dimensions. rpx calls variables.

title: Introduction to rpx
code: library(rpx)  # Known aliases scb <- px_api("scb", lang = "en") ssb <- px_api("ssb", lang = "en")  # Or a custom URL custom <- px_api("https://my.statbank.example/api/v2/", lang = "en")  # See all known APIs px_api_catalogue()

title: Introduction to rpx
text: Tables central entity. get_tables() sends server-side search query. result tibble rich metadata: table tibble includes subject path, time period range, time unit, data source — searchable table_search().
code: tables <- get_tables(scb, query = "income") |>   table_search("taxable")  tables |> table_describe(max_n = 3)

title: Introduction to rpx
text: table set variables (dimensions). key discovery step get_variables(): Important variable properties: - elimination: can variable left get_data() call? TRUE, omitting means API returns pre-computed total (e.g., omitting “Sex” gives total sexes). FALSE, variable mandatory — must include . - time: time dimension? - values: available codes human-readable labels. - codelists: alternative groupings (e.g. municipalities → counties).
code: vars <- get_variables(scb, "TAB638") vars |> variable_describe() # See what values a variable has vars |> variable_values("Kon")  # Look up variable codes by name variable_name_to_code(vars, "sex")

title: Introduction to rpx
text: know exactly want: Variables omit eliminated (aggregated) API allows . variable mandatory, must include .
code: pop <- get_data(scb, "TAB638",   Region = c("0180", "1480"),   Kon = c("1", "2"),   ContentsCode = "*",   Tid = px_top(5) )

title: Introduction to rpx
text: interactive exploration, prepare_query() inspects table metadata builds query sensible defaults: Default strategy: - ContentsCode: values ("*") - Time variable: latest 10 periods (px_top(10)) - Eliminable variables: omitted (API aggregates) - Small mandatory variables (≤ 22 values): ("*") - Large mandatory variables: first value (px_top(1)) Override specific variables letting defaults handle rest: maximize_selection = TRUE, function expands unspecified variables include many values possible staying API’s cell limit. fetch:
code: q <- prepare_query(scb, "TAB638") q <- prepare_query(scb, "TAB638",   Region = c("0180", "1480"),   maximize_selection = TRUE ) pop <- get_data(scb, query = q)

title: Introduction to rpx
text: sections cover features may need first query, become essential complex tables cross-country work.

title: Introduction to rpx
text: Codelists provide alternative groupings variable values. useful want data different aggregation level table’s default. example, “Region” variable 290 municipalities might codelist groups 21 counties:
code: cls <- get_codelists(scb, "TAB638", "Region") cls |> codelist_describe(max_n = 5)  # Use a codelist in a query get_data(scb, "TAB638",   Region = "*",   Tid = px_top(5),   ContentsCode = "*",   .codelist = list(Region = "vs_RegionLän07") )

title: Introduction to rpx
text: table multiple content variables (e.g. Population Deaths), use .output = "wide" pivot separate columns. useful want compute multiple measures (e.g. death rate = Deaths / Population):
code: demo <- get_data(scb, "TAB638",   Region = "0180",   Tid = px_top(5),   ContentsCode = "*",   .output = "wide" ) demo

title: Introduction to rpx
text: full control HTTP request — useful debugging need inspect/modify exact query sending — use low-level query composers:
code: q <- compose_data_query(scb, "TAB638",   Region = c("0180"),   ContentsCode = "*",   Tid = px_top(3) )  # Inspect the query q$url q$body  # Modify and execute raw <- execute_query(scb, q$url, q$body)

title: Introduction to rpx
text: PX-Web v2 supports server-side stored queries. Useful recurring reports — save query , retrieve ID later:
code: # Save a query id <- save_query(scb, "TAB638", Region = "0180", Tid = px_top(5), ContentsCode = "*")  # Retrieve later get_saved_query(scb, id)

title: Introduction to rpx
text: Always cite data sources:
code: px_cite(pop)

title: Working with multiple APIs
text: rpx ships catalogue known PX-Web instances. can also connect PX-Web API providing full URL.
code: library(rpx)  px_api_catalogue()

title: Working with multiple APIs
text: API object stores base URL, language, API version, configuration (cell limits, rate limits):
code: scb <- px_api("scb", lang = "en")      # Sweden (v2) ssb <- px_api("ssb", lang = "en")      # Norway (v2) statfi <- px_api("statfi", lang = "en") # Finland (v1) scb ssb

title: Working with multiple APIs
text: PX-Web two API versions: - v1: Legacy, POST-data queries, search endpoint. Table discovery requires walking folder hierarchy. - v2: Modern, GET+POST data queries, full-text search, codelists endpoint, saved queries. rpx handles versions transparently. user-facing functions signatures — internal request building differs. selection helpers v2-: px_bottom(), px_from(), px_to(), px_range() raise informative error used v1 API.

title: Working with multiple APIs
text: Suppose want compare population data across Sweden Norway. table IDs variable codes differ, workflow identical: Note table IDs completely different countries, variable names may also differ (“Region” SCB vs names elsewhere). Always run variable_describe() table building query:
code: library(dplyr) library(purrr)  # Find population tables in each country scb_tables <- get_tables(scb, query = "population") ssb_tables <- get_tables(ssb, query = "population")  # Explore a table from each scb_tables |> table_describe(max_n = 3) ssb_tables |> table_describe(max_n = 3) # Fetch data using prepare_query() for quick exploration scb_q <- prepare_query(scb, "TAB638",   Region = "00",       # "Riket" (whole country)   Tid = px_top(5),   ContentsCode = "BE0101N1" # Population )  # Norwegian table IDs are different — explore to find the right one ssb_vars <- get_variables(ssb, "05803") ssb_vars |> variable_describe()

title: Working with multiple APIs
text: Since get_data() returns standard tibbles table_id column, can bind results different APIs:
code: results <- list(   sweden = get_data(scb, query = scb_q),   norway = get_data(ssb, "05803",     ContentsCode = "Personer",     Tid = px_top(5)   ) )  # .id = "country" adds a column tracking which list element each row # came from — essential for traceability after binding bind_rows(results, .id = "country") # NOTE: column names may differ between countries. If so, you may need # to rename() before bind_rows() to align them.

title: Working with multiple APIs
text: Language matters. Codes often language-dependent. lang = "en" gives consistent labels across countries, codes table IDs language-independent. Table structure varies. Swedish tables may “Region” Finnish tables “Alue”. Run get_variables() |> variable_describe() table writing queries. API limits differ. SCB allows ~100 000 cells per request; agencies may allow less. Use api$config$max_cells check. prepare_query() respects limit automatically. v1 vs v2. agencies migrated v2. Selection helpers px_from(), px_range() etc. raise informative error used v1 API. Check api$version catalogue’s versions column.

Variable selections named arguments. name variable code, value one : character vector specific value codes (item selection) "*" values px_selections helper: px_all(), px_top(), px_bottom(), px_from(), px_to(), px_range() Omitted variables eliminated API allows Ignored query provided. query <px_query> object prepare_query(). provided, table_id, ..., .codelist taken query object. .codelist Named list codelist overrides (e.g. list(Region = "agg_RegionLan")). .output "long" (default, tidy) "wide" (pivot content variables). .comments Include footnotes/comments attribute. simplify Add human-readable text label columns alongside codes. auto_chunk Automatically split large queries exceed cell limit multiple requests. TRUE (default), variable values split batches, request staying limit. progress bar shown. Set FALSE error instead. max_results Override API's cell limit. set, value used instead limit reported API's config endpoint. title: Prepare a data query with smart defaults — prepare_query
text: Default selection strategy: ContentsCode: values ("*") Time variable: recent 10 periods (px_top(10)) Eliminable variables: omitted (API aggregates automatically) Small mandatory variables (<= max_default_values values): ("*") Large mandatory variables: first value (px_top(1)) maximize_selection = TRUE, function expands selections use much API's cell limit possible. Expansion order: smallest eliminable variables first, smallest mandatory, time last. returned query object prints human-readable summary showing selected variable . Modify selections passing get_data() assigning $selections list.

title: rpx 0.1.0
text: Initial CRAN release.

title: rpx 0.1.0
text: Improved quickstart vignette: explains ContentsCode, elimination, _text columns, adds inline ggplot comments. Improved introduction vignette: navigation help, clearer elimination explanation, “Advanced features” marker, motivation codelists/wide output/query composition/saved queries. Improved multi-api vignette: honest framing cross-country challenges, guidance finding comparable tables, actionable tips, cross-references. Fixed Unicode ≤ prepare_query() documentation caused LaTeX PDF manual errors.

title: rpx 0.1.0
text: API connection: px_api() creates reusable connection objects PX-Web API (SCB, SSB, Statistics Finland, etc.) automatic version detection (v1/v2). API availability: px_available() performs lightweight connectivity check, used guard examples tests. Table discovery: get_tables() searches lists available tables support v1 (tree-walking) v2 (search endpoint) APIs. Variable inspection: get_variables() retrieves variable metadata table, including value lists selection types. Data retrieval: get_data() downloads data automatic query construction, chunking large requests, rate limiting. Codelists: get_codelists() retrieves codelist metadata; codelist_values() codelist_describe() inspection. Selection helpers: px_all(), px_top(), px_bottom(), px_from(), px_to(), px_range() concise variable selections. Query workflow: prepare_query() interactive query building; compose_table_query(), compose_data_query(), execute_query() programmatic access. Saved queries: save_query() get_saved_query() persisting reloading query specifications. Table helpers: table_search(), table_describe(), table_enrich(), table_minimize(), table_extract_ids() working table metadata. Variable helpers: variable_search(), variable_describe(), variable_minimize(), variable_extract_ids(), variable_name_to_code(), variable_values() working variables. Data helpers: data_minimize(), data_comments(), data_legend() working downloaded data. Persistent caching: rpx_cache_dir() rpx_clear_cache() managing cached API responses using tools::R_user_dir(). Citations: px_cite() generates citations downloaded data. HTTP resilience: Automatic retry exponential backoff transient errors rate limiting (HTTP 429). Built-API catalogue: Ships catalogue known PX-Web APIs Nordic European statistical agencies.
